message:
refactor(e2e): add remote-stall fail-closed guards to 9 replay smoke harnesses (ft-aomsv)

Upgrade all remaining replay E2E harnesses that use `rch exec -- cargo`
without fail-closed guards. Each harness now includes:

- RCH preflight probe (workers reachable + smoke cargo check --help)
- Timeout-wrapped execution via timeout/gtimeout with SIGTERM+kill-after
- RCH-REMOTE-STALL detection (exit 124/137) with structured log + fatal
- RCH_FAIL_OPEN_REGEX check rejecting local fallback execution
- ensure_rch_ready() gate before first cargo operation

Files upgraded:
- test_replay_artifact_reader.sh
- test_replay_artifact_write_read.sh
- test_replay_capture_extraction.sh
- test_replay_decision_capture.sh
- test_replay_fixture_harvest.sh
- test_replay_kernel_determinism.sh
- test_replay_performance.sh
- test_replay_proptests.sh
- test_replay_redaction.sh

All existing test logic, structured JSON logging, and scenario
reporting preserved. All files pass bash -n syntax validation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+    set +e

+    local rc=$?
+    set -e
+    check_rch_fallback "${output_file}"

+        fatal "RCH-REMOTE-STALL: rch remote command timed out after ${RCH_STEP_TIMEOUT_SECS}s. See ${output_file}"
+    fi
+    return "${rc}"
+}
+
+ensure_rch_ready() {
+    if ! command -v rch >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        fatal "rch is required for this E2E harness; refusing local cargo execution."
+    fi
+    resolve_timeout_bin
+    if [[ -z "${TIMEOUT_BIN}" ]]; then
+        fatal "timeout or gtimeout is required to fail closed on stalled remote execution."
+    fi
+    set +e
+    run_rch --json workers probe --all >"${RCH_PROBE_LOG}" 2>&1
+    local probe_rc=$?
+    set -e
+    if [[ ${probe_rc} -ne 0 ]] || ! probe_has_reachable_workers "${RCH_PROBE_LOG}"; then
+        fatal "rch workers are unavailable; refusing local cargo execution. See ${RCH_PROBE_LOG}"
+    fi
+    set +e
+    run_rch_cargo_logged "${RCH_SMOKE_LOG}" env CARGO_TARGET_DIR="${cargo_target_dir}" cargo check --help
+    local smoke_rc=$?
+    set -e
+    if [[ ${smoke_rc} -ne 0 ]]; then

+    fi
+}
